thesis: DMN is a decision-system entry point. Faithful rendering of system dynamics requires a validated semantic graph backbone with provenance, temporal state overlays, and reproducible view manifests.

view_scales.label: Overview
view_scales.description: System-level purpose, backbone, and major modeling families.

view_scales.label: Domain
view_scales.description: Standards and modeling systems around the entry point.

view_scales.label: Artifact
view_scales.description: Concrete model artifacts, interchange formats, and contracts.

view_scales.label: Runtime
view_scales.description: Execution surfaces, event streams, telemetry, and service boundaries.

view_scales.label: Evidence
view_scales.description: Observations, provenance, validation results, and rendered view records.

layers.label: Layer Up
layers.description: Process, case, enterprise, systems, and runtime architecture context above DMN.

layers.label: DMN Layer
layers.description: Decision modeling concepts and directly adjacent decision semantics.

layers.label: Layer Down
layers.description: Serialization, execution, model artifacts, APIs, and rule/runtime targets below DMN.

layers.label: Semantic Core
layers.description: Canonical graph identity, validation, provenance, and exchange contracts.

layers.label: Observation And State
layers.description: State machines, event logs, telemetry, traces, and event envelopes.

layers.label: Rendering
layers.description: View manifests and visualization engines that project the graph.

nodes.label: DMN
nodes.summary: Decision Model and Notation, the entry point for the map.
nodes.context: DMN models decision requirements and executable decision logic. It is valuable for decision dependencies, but it is too narrow to be the universal representation for topology, state, telemetry, and provenance.
hardening: Treat DMN decisions as one claim type in the graph, not as the global model.
Preserve DMN version, namespace, source document, and import provenance.

nodes.label: DRD / DRG
nodes.summary: DMN decision requirement diagrams and decision requirement graphs.
nodes.context: DRDs show dependency between decisions, input data, business knowledge, and knowledge sources. They are not runtime event graphs.
hardening: Do not infer process sequence or runtime causality from a DRD edge without evidence.

nodes.label: Decision Service
nodes.summary: A callable packaging boundary for decisions.
nodes.context: Decision services expose decisions for invocation by process, case, application, or runtime architecture components.
hardening: Separate design-time decision service boundaries from deployed API endpoints.

nodes.label: Decision Table
nodes.summary: Tabular decision logic used by DMN.
nodes.context: Decision tables are common DMN logic artifacts and often become executable rule assets.
hardening: Track hit policy, type constraints, FEEL expressions, and test coverage.

nodes.label: FEEL
nodes.summary: Friendly Enough Expression Language used by DMN.
nodes.context: FEEL is the expression language side of DMN. Runtime portability depends on precise FEEL support and version handling.
hardening: Record FEEL dialect/version support for each runtime before marking a decision executable.

nodes.label: SBVR
nodes.summary: Business vocabulary and rule semantics adjacent to DMN.
nodes.context: SBVR is relevant when vocabulary precision and rule semantics need to be connected to decision logic.
hardening: Use only where controlled vocabulary improves precision; avoid importing a parallel semantics stack by habit.
aliases: Semantics of Business Vocabulary and Rules

nodes.label: BPMN
nodes.summary: Business Process Model and Notation for process flow.
nodes.context: BPMN process models can invoke, route to, or depend on decisions. It gives process context above DMN.
hardening: Do not treat BPMN sequence flow as proof of actual runtime ordering without event evidence.

nodes.label: CMMN
nodes.summary: Case Management Model and Notation for case plans.
nodes.context: CMMN covers less prescriptive, case-oriented work that can use decisions without fitting a strict process flow.
hardening: Represent discretionary case behavior separately from deterministic process paths.

nodes.label: ArchiMate
nodes.summary: Enterprise architecture modeling language.
nodes.context: ArchiMate situates decisions in business, application, technology, motivation, strategy, and implementation context.
hardening: Use ArchiMate for enterprise context, not for low-level runtime state.

nodes.label: SysML v2
nodes.summary: Systems modeling language for MBSE.
nodes.context: SysML v2 covers system requirements, structure, behavior, analysis, and verification context around decision-enabled systems.
hardening: Keep engineering requirement/verification evidence distinct from business decision dependencies.

nodes.label: C4 / Runtime Topology
nodes.summary: Software architecture topology around deployed decision services.
nodes.context: Runtime topology explains which services, stores, queues, and users interact with a decision service.
hardening: Treat runtime topology as observed or configured infrastructure evidence, not as a DMN extension.

nodes.label: DMN XML / DI
nodes.summary: DMN interchange and diagram information.
nodes.context: DMN XML and diagram interchange preserve model content and layout metadata across tools.
hardening: Preserve namespace, schema version, diagram coordinates, and original bytes hash.

nodes.label: Rule Engine
nodes.summary: Executable target for decision rules.
nodes.context: Decision tables and FEEL expressions may be interpreted, compiled, or translated into a rule/runtime engine.
hardening: Declare runtime conformance, unsupported FEEL features, and test result provenance.

nodes.label: Predictive Model Artifacts
nodes.summary: PMML, ONNX, PFA-class analytical artifacts adjacent to decisions.
nodes.context: Predictive models often feed decisions or sit beside them. They need separate lineage, validation, and runtime semantics.
hardening: Do not collapse model score provenance into decision-rule provenance.

nodes.label: Decision Runtime API
nodes.summary: Callable runtime boundary for decision evaluation.
nodes.context: The deployed decision API is where process, applications, telemetry, traces, and event envelopes meet decision logic.
hardening: Expose versioned decision IDs, input schema, output schema, model hash, and evaluation trace IDs.

nodes.label: RDF / OWL Knowledge Graph
nodes.summary: Canonical identity and relationship backbone.
nodes.context: The semantic graph is the durable center: it represents identities, relations, named graph partitions, and ontology commitments across model families.
hardening: Use named graphs for asserted, inferred, observed, simulated, and rendered claims.
Map source-model identifiers to stable IRIs.

nodes.label: SHACL Contracts
nodes.summary: Graph validation shapes and quality gates.
nodes.context: SHACL constrains the graph before data or projections are treated as trustworthy.
hardening: Validate relation vocabulary, required provenance, status values, temporal bounds, and render-manifest completeness.

nodes.label: PROV-O
nodes.summary: Provenance ontology for agents, activities, and entities.
nodes.context: PROV-O records where a claim came from, who or what generated it, and which source or transformation it depends on.
hardening: Require provenance for imports, inferred edges, simulated states, and rendered views.

nodes.label: JSON-LD / TriG
nodes.summary: Portable graph and named graph exchange formats.
nodes.context: JSON-LD is useful for web-facing exchange. TriG is useful when named graph partitions need to be serialized directly.
hardening: Version contexts and keep view data derivable from canonical graph snapshots.

nodes.label: Temporal State / Events
nodes.summary: Pattern for representing state as time-bounded evidence.
nodes.context: Topology and state should not be conflated. Current state is derived from observations and events with timestamps, source, confidence, and expiry.
hardening: Represent state transitions and observed state separately from static model structure.

nodes.label: SCXML Statecharts
nodes.summary: State machine notation for control abstraction.
nodes.context: SCXML is relevant for executable or exchangeable state machine behavior, but it is not the full graph backbone.
hardening: Use for explicit state-machine behavior; do not generalize it into all topology.

nodes.label: XES Event Log
nodes.summary: Event log format for process mining.
nodes.context: XES-style logs can provide process/event evidence for observed behavior and conformance checks.
hardening: Keep event log evidence separate from designed process or decision models.

nodes.label: OpenTelemetry
nodes.summary: Telemetry conventions for traces, metrics, logs, profiles, and resources.
nodes.context: OpenTelemetry gives runtime observation vocabulary. It should feed observed graph overlays, not overwrite the design graph.
hardening: Attach telemetry to runtime identities with time bounds, resource identity, sampling metadata, and confidence.

nodes.label: Trace Context
nodes.summary: Distributed trace propagation format.
nodes.context: Trace Context helps correlate observed runtime behavior across service boundaries.
hardening: Use trace IDs as evidence correlation keys, not canonical architectural identity.

nodes.label: CloudEvents
nodes.summary: Common event metadata envelope.
nodes.context: CloudEvents-style envelopes can normalize event metadata flowing into the observation graph.
hardening: Preserve original event payload, envelope attributes, source, subject, and schema references.

nodes.label: View Manifest
nodes.summary: Reproducible contract for a rendered projection.
nodes.context: Every rendered visualization should declare graph snapshot, filters, aggregation rules, scale, layout engine, hidden layers, and generation provenance.
hardening: Treat rendered views as derived artifacts with their own provenance and validation status.

nodes.label: Cytoscape.js
nodes.summary: Interactive graph visualization engine for the v0 map.
nodes.context: Cytoscape.js provides graph elements, styles, layouts, and event handlers suitable for an exploratory topology viewer.
hardening: Keep Cytoscape element data generated from the graph contract and view manifest.

nodes.label: React Flow
nodes.summary: Node-edge editor candidate for future authoring workflows.
nodes.context: React Flow is better suited to graph editing and node configuration workflows than to very large exploratory topology maps.
hardening: Use for controlled authoring surfaces; validate edits before committing to the semantic graph.

nodes.label: Sigma.js
nodes.summary: Large graph rendering candidate for simpler graph exploration.
nodes.context: Sigma is relevant when the graph grows large and needs fast rendering of simpler node-link structures.
hardening: Use only after defining aggregation and sampling contracts for large views.

edges.summary: DMN includes decision requirement diagrams/graphs.
DMN uses FEEL for expressions.
DMN can package decision behavior as decision services.
Decision tables are common DMN logic artifacts.
SBVR can provide controlled business vocabulary and rule semantics adjacent to DMN.
Business processes can invoke or depend on decisions.
Case management contexts can use decisions without strict process sequencing.
Enterprise architecture provides capability and application context for decisions.
Systems models can contextualize decisions within requirements, behavior, and verification.
Runtime architecture contains deployed decision API components.
DMN models are interchanged as XML with diagram information.
Decision tables may be executed by rule engines.
Executable decision logic depends on runtime FEEL support.
A design-time decision service can be implemented as a deployed API.
Predictive artifacts can feed or complement decision logic.
DMN identities and relationships should be lifted into the canonical semantic graph.
Process models should map into the same identity graph rather than stay isolated.
Enterprise architecture context should share identity with decision and runtime nodes.
Systems models should map into the same backbone for cross-resolution analysis.
Graph snapshots should pass shape validation before trusted rendering.
Graph claims need provenance for imports, mappings, observations, and render outputs.
Graph snapshots should have portable exchange forms.
Temporal state/event evidence overlays the canonical identity graph.
Explicit state-machine behavior can be represented with SCXML-like statecharts.
Event logs provide observed behavior evidence for process and decision analysis.
Runtime decision services should emit telemetry that can be correlated to decisions and topology.
Trace Context correlates runtime evaluation behavior across service calls.
Decision services may emit or consume event envelopes for integration.
Telemetry evidence should attach to canonical runtime identities.
Rendered views should be generated from graph snapshots through view manifests.
View manifests should be generated only from validated graph snapshots.
View generation should itself be a provenance-bearing activity.
The v0 dynamic map is rendered with Cytoscape.js.
Future graph authoring or workflow editing could use React Flow.
Very large simple graph projections may use Sigma.
